label: Byblos (Ancient Gebal)
sub: One of world's oldest cities; UNESCO site; Phoenician alphabet origin

label: Tyre (Sur)
sub: Ancient Phoenician capital; UNESCO site; scene of Alexander's 332 BCE siege

label: Sidon (Saida)
sub: Major Phoenician city-state; Crusader Sea Castle; Palestinian refugee camp Ain el-Hilweh nearby

label: Baalbek (Heliopolis)
sub: Largest Roman temple complex; UNESCO site; Hezbollah stronghold in Bekaa Valley

label: Beirut
sub: Capital; political, cultural, and economic centre; severely damaged in 2006 war and 2020 explosion

label: Beirut Port
sub: Site of Aug 4, 2020 ammonium nitrate explosion; 217 killed, 7,000+ injured; investigation blocked

label: Sabra & Shatila Camps
sub: Palestinian refugee camps; site of Sep 1982 Phalangist massacre of 800–3,500 civilians

label: Martyrs' Square
sub: Focal point of 2005 Cedar Revolution and 2019 Thawra protests demanding Syrian withdrawal and reform

label: Hariri Assassination Site
sub: St George Hotel Beirut Corniche; PM Rafik Hariri killed Feb 14, 2005 by car bomb (1,800 kg)

label: Dahiyeh (South Beirut)
sub: Hezbollah stronghold; heavily bombed in 2006 war and Sep 27, 2024 strike killing Nasrallah

label: Tripoli
sub: Lebanon's second city; predominantly Sunni; site of Nahr el-Bared camp battle 2007; bombed 2024

label: Nahr el-Bared Camp
sub: Palestinian refugee camp; 2007 Lebanese army siege of Fatah al-Islam; 418 killed, camp destroyed

label: Naqoura — UNIFIL HQ
sub: UN Interim Force in Lebanon headquarters; Blue Line monitoring; 10,500 troops from 50+ nations

label: Marjayoun
sub: Mixed Christian-Shia town; Israeli Security Zone administrative centre 1985–2000; conflict area 2024

label: Khiam Detention Centre
sub: Israeli-backed South Lebanon Army prison 1985–2000; torture documented; liberated May 2000; bombed 2006

label: Beaufort Castle (Qal'at Shaqif)
sub: Crusader fortress 12th c.; held by PLO, then IDF 1982; used by Hezbollah; strategically significant

label: Qana (Cana)
sub: Site of Apr 18, 1996 UNIFIL compound shelling (106 killed); second massacre Jul 30, 2006 (28 killed)

label: Zahle
sub: Largest Christian city in Bekaa Valley; besieged by Syria 1981-82; gateway to Bekaa Valley

label: Lebanese Parliament (Nejmeh Square)
sub: National legislature; seat of confessional power; Joseph Aoun elected president here Jan 9, 2025

label: Grand Serail (Government HQ)
sub: Prime Minister's office; Ottoman-era building; seat of executive power in Beirut city centre

label: Baabda Presidential Palace
sub: Residence of the President of Lebanon; where French General Gouraud proclaimed Greater Lebanon Sep 1, 1920

label: Ain el-Rummaneh
sub: Beirut neighbourhood; site of Apr 13, 1975 bus massacre that triggered the civil war

label: Banque du Liban
sub: Lebanese central bank; accused of financial engineering that contributed to 2019–2020 collapse; Riad Salameh era

label: Nabatieh
sub: Shia city in South Lebanon; Hezbollah stronghold; heavily bombed in 2006 war and 2024 conflict

label: Jounieh
sub: Christian heartland north of Beirut; port used by Maronite militias during civil war; Free Patriotic Movement base

label: Deir el-Qamar
sub: Historic Druze-Maronite mountain village; Fakhr al-Din II capital; 1860 civil war Druze-Maronite violence

label: Ain el-Hilweh Camp
sub: Largest Palestinian refugee camp in Lebanon (Sidon); pop. ~90,000; Lebanese army cannot enter; frequent armed clashes

label: Bint Jbeil
sub: 'Capital of South Lebanon'; site of fierce fighting in 2006 war and IDF ground ops Oct 2024

label: Tripoli Citadel (St Gilles)
sub: Crusader castle built 1103 by Raymond of Toulouse; the remains of the County of Tripoli's seat

label: Beit Eddine Palace
sub: 19th c. Emir Bashir II palace; museum and summer festival; Chouf Mountains; Druze political heartland
